name: DiRAC - Data-Intensive (CSD3)
oneLiner: DiRAC's Cambridge data-intensive service, optimised for high-throughput scientific datasets and GPU-rich workflows.
summary: The Cambridge DiRAC data-intensive service runs on the CSD3 platform and is designed for data-rich workloads in astrophysics and related fields. The published DiRAC summary highlights a combination of large CPU and GPU core counts, substantial RAM, and petabyte-scale storage, all connected by high-speed HDR networking. In AI terms, it is a good fit for inference pipelines that combine large datasets with GPU acceleration and heavy preprocessing.

keyFacts.hardware: Data-intensive service on CSD3 with large CPU and GPU estate, 157 TB memory, and 8 PB storage
keyFacts.performance: Around 30,412 CPU cores and 746,496 GPU cores
keyFacts.energy: Operated as part of Cambridge's shared high-performance infrastructure

access.route: Access is governed through the DiRAC Resource Allocation Committee and aligned with DiRAC's mission areas.
